titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Edward Lear Archive
titlestmt.author: compiled by Francis Lapka

publicationstmt.publisher: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

address.addressline: Department of Rare Books and Manuscripts

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday March 2, 2020 at 22:01

corpname.part: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

did.physdesc: 2 boxes
did.abstract: The collection comprises correspondence, manuscript writings, lists of drawings, and printed material concerning the author and artist Edward Lear.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The collection is the physical property of the Yale Center for British Art. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the Curator of Rare Books and Manuscripts.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Edward Lear Archive. Yale Center for British Art, Gift of Donald C. Gallup, Yale BA 1934, PhD 1939

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Donald C. Gallup, Yale BA 1934, PhD 1939

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged into four series: I. Correspondence; II. Manuscript writings; III. Lists of drawings by Edward Lear; IV. Printed material

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material: For records and images of nine oil paintings and over 350 drawings and prints by Edward Lear also included in Donald Gallup's gift to the Yale Center for British Art, see the YCBA's online catalog.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Gallup's collecting of Edward Lear material is described in: Gallup, Donald C. "Collecting Edward Lear." The Yale University Library Gazette 61, no. 3/4 (1987): 125-42. http://www.jstor.org/stable/40858913.
And in: Wilcox, Scott. Edward Lear and the art of travel. New Haven: Yale Center for British Art, 2000.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Better known as the author of "The Owl and the Pussy-Cat" and other nonsense verse, Edward Lear (1812-1888) was also one of the great artist travelers of the Victorian age. Although Lear suffered from epilepsy, asthma, poor eyesight, and chronic depression, he was an inveterate traveler and an indefatigable sketcher, documenting a lifetime of journeys throughout the Mediterranean and India.

title.part: Moore, Thomas, 1779-1852. By that lake, whose gloomy shore

did.unittitle: Edward Lear letter to Ann Lear
did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet (4 pages) : autograph letter, signed ; 27 x 43 cm, folded to 27 x 22 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lear writes to his sister Ann, from "the desert: outside the walls of Suez." He begins with a lengthy discussion of camel travel, pleasant as, "you are lifted up on the long necked monster--& away you go just as if on a rocking chair." And yet they are quite irritable: "when I put the vegetable within a yard of him [Lear's camel], he yells & grunts as if I were killing him….If you try to make them go faster—they grown: if you stop them or try to go slower—they growl also." He describes the tents he stays in—very comfortable—as well as his hope to see pilgrims on their way to Mecca. Lear expresses his appreciation for the desert, writing that "these symptoms of civilization [i.e., beer, etc.] rather spoil the lonely character of the wide desert." The letter spans multiple days, and later on Lear gives descriptions of his Arab travelling companions: he praises Ibrahim as sharp, quick, and a good cook; and says "2 are 'blackymoors'—(but you know I never liked blackies, though you did make me walk round that chimneysweeper 33 years ago.)" Lear ends up missing the pilgrims to Mecca, and instead travels elsewhere, through mixed weather. He intersperses his account with references to scripture, such as that "it is not known how the Israelites came up to Mt. Sinai—but perhaps by Wady Feiran." Lear references a "curious dance—but I must describe that at some other time," and a visit to Mt. Sinai. He ends his letter near Suez, mentioning that he hopes to be in Jerusalem soon and that, "you would not know me if you saw me, as I have a beard not as well as Mustaches." He includes instructions to send the letter, or perhaps something else enclosed, to a Robert A. Hornby, Esq. The letter includes a tiny sketch of several figures and a camel.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink. Accompanied by a typescript transcription of the letter. This letter is transcribed in its entirety in Edward Lear: Selected Letters, edited by Vivien Noakes.

did.unittitle: Edward Lear letter to Ann Lear
did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet (4 pages) : autograph letter, signed ; 29 x 41 cm, folded to 29 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lear writes to his sister Ann, describing his travels through the Eastern Mediterranean. He opens his letter with an apology—for his letters "miserably shabby as they are"—and proceeds to describe his daily travel routine, slow due to his desire to sketch what he sees. He describes the topography of Lebanon, and mentions a Mr. and Mrs. Kilgour, traveling companions and "exceedingly good American people." At times, "the cold was so great I could not hold my graphite well," frustrating his desire to sketch the regions cedars, though "it is quite a pleasure to be in these mountains, away from [those] hateful Arabs: - here every one speaks to you & is friendly." The letter soon breaks, picking up at another time (5PM). Lear writes about his continued travels, as well as those with him, including Giorgio, Lear's longtime servant, who "is always most good & valuable." Lear notes the impending start to the hajj, and expresses his eagerness to see it. Lear laments that he has "totally given up all idea of crossing the Jordan, for the Arabs have just bust out a fighting between themselves! - & I heartily hope they will all kill each other." Lear does, however, speak positively about "the splendor of costume," writing that "10 Syrians together would blind all the Maccaws in the Zoological Garden." He soon ends the letter, only to begin again, writing that, "my dear, - if you want a Dog – now is your time: - this city is quite full of them: they lie in heaps of 10 or 20." He finishes with his plans to sail directly to Corfu following the completion of his journey.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in graphite. Accompanied by a typescript transcription of the letter.

did.unittitle: Edward Lear letter to unidentified recipient
did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet (4 pages) : autograph letter, signed ; 19 x 24 cm, folded to 19 x 12 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lear writes to a "sir," asking that he review the attached advertisement for views, "in the Ionian Islands," and offer assistance publicizing the series. The letter is written on a blank page of a prospectus for "Views in the Seven Ionian Islands; by Edward Lear" and includes a list of subscribers "already obtained."

title.part: Lear, Edward, 1812-1888. Views in the seven Ionian islands

did.unittitle: Edward Lear letter to James Fields
did.physdesc: 1 sheet (2 pages) : autograph letter, signed ; 14 x 13 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lear writes to James Fields, apologizing for not sending the "Curtis Phila sketch," and explaining that "Mr. Bush" will ask Fields what the possibilities are for the "Corsica or Xmas book in America." He closes with a wish to see Fields in Boston.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink. Pasted (by a later owner) to a sheet of gray paper, 22 x 18 cm.

did.unittitle: Edward Lear letter to Edmund Woodthorpe
did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet (3 pages) : autograph letter, signed ; 18 x 23 cm, folded to 18 x 12 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lear, his plans having fallen through, writes to Edmund Woodthorpe to suggest he come to Woodthorpe's house "in the wilderness," and to tell him that he will leave a copy "my last Book of Nonsense" for him at the specified address.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink. On paper watermarked "Joynson Fine."

did.unittitle: Edward Lear letter to Mr. (?) Dyer
did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet (4 pages) : autograph letter, signed ; 14 x 21 cm, folded to 14 x 11 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lear writes to a Mr. Dyer asking that they leave a frame where he might retrieve it. He remembers Mr. Dyer's purchase of a drawing and refers him to another—"Morn Broadened on the Borders of the Dark."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, on black-bordered mourning paper with watermark "J D L & Co."

did.unittitle: Edward Lear letter to Miss Cobden
did.physdesc: 1 sheet (2 pages) : autograph letter, signed ; 11 x 9 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lear remembers a past meeting with Miss Cobden and arranges for a future meeting, saying he will show her some drawings, including possibility "Damascus." No watermark.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.unittitle: Edward Lear letter to Annie Adams Fields
did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet (4 pages) : autograph letter ; 21 x 27 cm, folded to 21 x 14 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lear writes to Fields with condolences for the death of her husband, James Thomas Fields. He mentions that he has recently received several papers describing Fields's life, and he asks that she convey her thanks to the people who sent them (though he is not sure who that is). Lear closes his letter, writing, "you have the memories of golden hours passed with him who is gone for a time. So that in writing these lines I feel less sad than I might do in many similar cases."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink. Accompanied by a typescript transcription of the letter.

did.unittitle: Edward Lear letter to Hallam Tennyson
did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet (4 pages) : autograph letter, signed ; 21 x 27 cm, folded to 21 x 14 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lear writes from Villa Tennyson in Sanremo to Hallam Tennyson, at first apologizing for the delay, explaining that the letter traveled quite too far, due to "my frequent change of place owing to ill health, uncomfortable or unsuitable quarters, - quarantines, cordons, & whatnot." Lear notes that the previous letter included a poem, and "as you tell me to criticize, I must do so." His criticism of the poem, however, is lighthearted: he first explains that maize does not grow in this part of Italy—which the poem describes—and therefore "your allusion to Maize would cause only AMaizement to those who know this part of Italy." He later uses the romantic depictions of the region to launch into a discussion of its recent changes, writing, "its rocky promontories [are] all hacked & tunneled into monotonous walls, its trees cut down & replaced by lodging houses & warehouses." Lear advises Hallam not to visit, as the danger of cholera "make[s] travelling a bore" and as his own health is not good. He is, however, busy with illustrations, and he closes with a quotation from Alfred Tennyson.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.unittitle: Edward Lear letter to an unidentified recipient
did.physdesc: 1 sheet (1 page) : autograph letter, signed ; 11 x 14 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lear writes to an unidentified recipient that "I do not know tht I can add anythg more." He then asks whether there is any chance of him seeing the recipient soon.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.unittitle: W.H. Auden letter to Simon Nowell-Smith
did.physdesc: 1 sheet (1 page) : autograph letter, signed ; 26 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Auden thanks Nowell-Smith for the proofs of Auden's poem, "Edward Lear," for publication in the March 25, 1939, issue of the Times Literary Supplement (Spring Books supplement). Auden requests that the line "his welcome was prodigious: a flower took his hat" be changed to read "How prodigious the welcome was: flowers took his hat." The correction was not made.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in blue ink. No watermark. Addressed from: Hotel George Washington, 23rd Street & Lexington Ave, N.Y.C.

did.unittitle: St. Kiven and the Gentle Kathleen : manuscript
did.physdesc: 8 sheets : ink drawings, autograph manuscript ; 12 x 19 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comprises the verse of Thomas Moore's "By That Lake, Whose Gloomy Shore" from vol. 4 of his Irish Melodies, illustrated by original drawings by Lear. The verse describes the story of Saint Kevin (of Glendalough?) who responds to the love of his devoted admirer only after he has cruelly cast her to her death. The sheets are numbered 1-9; sheet 5 is lacking.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Text and drawings In black ink.

title.part: Moore, Thomas, 1779-1852. By that lake, whose gloomy shore

did.physdesc: 1 sheet : autograph poem ; 28 x 22 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten copy of W.H. Auden's (1907-1973) poem, "Edward Lear," originally published in 1939. The poem refers to Lear's "Terrible Demon," likely his poor health, as well as his time in Italy, his love of children, his hatred of his own nose, and his love of his cat, Foss ("a cat invited him to dance and shyly squeezed his hand").

did.unittitle: Lists of drawings by Edward Lear

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 10 entries by date ranging from July 18 to October 20, 1837.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written primarily in black ink, with additions in graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on 22 drawings, places, and dates, made in the vicinity of Naples, Italy, February 6 to October 6, 1838.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 17 mounts / 24 drawings made in the vicinity of Naples, Italy, August 9-27, 1838.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite. Watermark: "Houghton and Gunn, 162 Bond St."

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 42 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 78 drawings, with notes in graphite concerning an additional 20. From March 2, 1839, to October 28, 1840.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 41 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Page 1 begins with a list of six drawings from April 13 to May 10, 1842. The rest of pages 1 and 2 list 63 mounts / 80 drawings from May 8 to July 15, 1847.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, on ruled paper.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet, 1 envelope ; 10 x 15 cm

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The slip bears sparse notes on the Sicily drawings of 1842 and 1847. The envelope (re-used, apparently) was originally addressed to Lushington.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 23 x 37 cm, folded to 23 x 19 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 38 mounts (unnumbered) / 55 drawings, July 26 to November 3, 1847.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 31 x 22 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily a rough draft of Lear's list for 1863 (see folder 30 of the present collection). Also includes a section on 1848 drawings.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in graphite, with additions in black ink.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 29 mounts from Lear's 1848 and 1863 visits, primarily the latter.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in some graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 41 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A draft list, prepatory for list in folder 21a of in the present collection. Though numbered 1-100, only 38 entries are completed.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite. On blue paper, watermarked: "E. Towgood 1864."

did.physdesc: 20 pages (some blank) ; 33 x 41 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two lists, in one gathering. The first is titled "Ionian Isles 1848" and occupies the first 3 leaves. With 134 listed drawings (two left blank), April 24 to May 28. Annotated below caption title: "Please return with drawings." Followed by two blank leaves.

The second list, across five leaves, is captioned "Greece 1848." Enumerates drawings up to 134 (five of which have 'A' and 'B', and three of which are blank), from June 1 to July 27. The enumeration continues to leaves 4 and 5, though no drawings are listed. On the 4th leaf "Greece 1848" is crossed out, replaced with "Constantinople 1848."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 40 cm, folded to 33 x 20 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Apparently a rough draft of the Greece list described in folder 21 of this collection (here item 134 is Cape Colonia whereas in the related list item 134 is Athens).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite. On blue paper, watermarked: "E Towgood 1864."

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 16 x 21 cm

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, on ruled paper. The bottom portion of the sheet has been torn away.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 11 drawings, from August 17 to September 9, 1848.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 32 x 41 cm, folded to 32 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A partial list, enumerated to 111 but with many blanks. Lists 39 mounts, from January 10 to February 5.. Seven drawings noted as "to Ld. Derby."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite graphite.

persname.part: Derby, Edward Smith Stanley, Earl of, 1775-1851

did.physdesc: 20 pages (some blank) ; 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: There are two lists within this gathering. The first is titled "Greece 1849" and occupies the first five. It lists 209 drawings, from March 8 to April 23, 1849. With Lear's note by the title: "Lists 4-5, please return drawings."
The next two leaves are titled "Albania 1849," listing 83 drawings, May 1-28, 1849.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite. The paper is watermarked "WCB & Co. London 1848."

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 41 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The list is numbered to 82 (with blanks), largely replicating the Albania list in folder 25 of the collection. No dates are recorded.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite. Watermark: "Joynson.".

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 9 x 23 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The front, in black ink, summarizes: "130 mounts. 14 Ionian mounts." The verso, in graphite, tabulates mounts for Malta (138) and Greece (130).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink and graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 31 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Summarizes several sequences of mounts / drawings.
At top: "Case 4. Malta & Gozo 1866. Mounted & unmounted. 138 drawings in all. Morea & North Greece 1849. 130 mounts (138 + 130 = 268)"
At bottom: "Case 5. Crete, Corfu. English ... 184 mounts."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.physdesc: 3 sheets ; 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Egypt no. 1 lists 80 mounts, from December 29, 1853, to February 12, 1854. Egypt no. 2 lists 71 mounts from February 12 to March 18, 1853.
Accompanied by four smaller sheets summarizing sequences of the Egypt 1854 drawings: 36-67, 83-99, 100-122, and 123-151.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 17 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 13 mounts (no. 10 is blank), August 8-23, 1856.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite. Paper appears to be torn and list begins on the extant bottom half.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 41 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 76 mounts / 124 drawings, from August 8 to October 4, 1856. The first 13 mounts replicate the list of the "Across Albania August 1856" list (folder 29).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite. On blue paper.

did.physdesc: 2 sheets ; 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of 64 entries (with 40 numbered mounts), from January (?) 7 to April 22, 1857. Note at top: "Mounts - 40 (all which Lear sent me)"

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 41 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of 68 mounts (unnumbered) / 102 drawings (numbered), from January 7 to March 7 and then April 2-22, 1857. Includes notes on clients.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink. On blue paper.

did.physdesc: 2 folded sheets ; 33 x 41 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 1 lists mounts 1-65 (13 are blank), from March 20 to April 27, 1858.
No. 2 lists mounts 66-140 (12 are blank, and 52-65 are duplicated at beginning), from April 27 to June 5, 1858.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 41 cm, folded to 33 x 21

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists mounts 1-57, duplicating the same numbers in the "No. 1" list (folder 33), with variations in place names and dates.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 34 x 41 cm, folded to 34 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 36 mounts / 43 drawings, May 12-22, 1860. On the back of the sheet is a list of place names in Egypt and Italy, and the word "sunset."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink. Watermark: "Fellows 1865".

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rough notes describing approximately 35 drawings, from March 20 to May 13.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 40 cm, folded to 33 x 20 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 181 mounts / 215 drawings, from April to May 30, 1863.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink. On blue paper. Watermark: "E Towgood 1864".

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 33 x 20 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two lists, both relating to Corfu. The first, on the recto, is titled "1864" and comprises a rough list of 11 drawings, from January 26 to April 2, 1864.
The second, on the verso, is titled "1864" and comprises a rough list of 9 drawings, April 18-21, 1866.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 42 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 126 entries (probably mounts), April 13 to June 6, 1864.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 21 x 13 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The list lacks a heading, and the drawings are unnumbered and undated. The places identified are in Crete, and the verso bears the text of a journal entry in Crete, crossed out.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink. Blue paper, with watermark: "E Towgood 1864."

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 40 cm, folded to 33 x 20 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 103 mounts / 147 drawings, December 6-31, 1864.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink. On blue paper. Watermark: "E Towgood 1864."

did.physdesc: 3 folded sheets ; 32 x 41 cm, folded to 32 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 111 mounts (1-8 and 1-103, with two blanks). Largely a copy of the "Riviera di Ponente, Corniche 1864" list (folder 41), with variations in place names, November 16 to December 4 and December 6-31, 1864.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 32 x 41 cm, folded to 32 x 21 cm

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 61 mounts, from January 15 to April 10, 1865.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 37 mounts (with one blank), from November 10 to December 2, 1865.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite.

did.physdesc: 2 folded sheets ; 33 x 40 cm, folded to 33 x 20 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 83 mounts / 289 drawings, from December 24, 1865, to April 7, 1866.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite. On blue paper: "E Towgood 1864."

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 40 cm, folded to 33 x 20 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 27 drawings, April 18-21, 1866.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, on blue paper. Watermark: "E Towgood 1864."

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 40 cm, folded to 33 x 20 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 58 drawings, from April 21 to May 9, 1866.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, on blue paper watermarked: "E Towgood 1864."

did.physdesc: 2 folded sheets ; sheets 33 x 41 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The first folded sheet comprises mounts 1-80 from box 1 (items 1-63) and box 2 (items 64-80), from December 31, 1866, to February 3, 1867.
The second folded sheet comprises mounts from box 2 (items 81-120) and box 3 (items 121-166), from Februrary 4 to March 15, 1867.
Accompanied by three smaller slips concerning the same sequences.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists mounts 167-179, with an additional 12 unnumbered mounts, from March 22 to April 24, 1867.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite.

did.unittitle: North Italy 1867 : Palestine, Philistia, Jerusalem in 1867
did.physdesc: 8 pages ; 29 x 20 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two lists. The first, titled "North Italy 1867" (pages 1-6), lists 234 drawings, from May 3 to June 2, 1867.
The second, titled "Palestine, Philistia, Jerusalem in 1867" (page 7), lists 41 drawings (1-5 and 38-41 are blank), April 3-14.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 41 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 58 items, from May 3 to June 2.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 33 x 41 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 13 mounts, with varied dates, 1868-1869.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.unittitle: List i.e. number of drawings (mounts) left -- after the cases sent to England ...
did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 31 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: After "... sent to England" the caption continues "-- can gone [?] away (unmounted dwgs not included)"
The list names 30 journeys, most with dates, and with the number of mounts remaining (from 23 to 250).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite.

did.unittitle: Drawings and Paintings. Villa Tennyson Sanremo. December 1, 1881.
did.physdesc: 20 pages ; 31 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inventory includes five distinct lists and five sheets of miscellaneous notes at the end.
Pages 3-14 list drawings, organized by size (A.1, B.2, C.3, and D.4).

Page 15 begins with the caption "Paintings 1883-1882." The remaining pages contained varied short lists. There are numerous references to drawings "sent to Foord's" (i.e. Foord & Dickinson, London) on certain dates.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 31 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The page is divided into two columns. The column at left is titled "Size C. No. 3" and lists 35 items; the column at right is titled "Size D. No.4" and lists 34 items.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet ; 32 x 41 cm, folded to 321 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The top portion of sheet lists watercolors; the bottom, oils. Includes references to prices, in pounds.
129 Wardour Street was the address of Foord & Dickinson.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 31 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Undated. Includes note at top right: "X means not to be framed yet."
The top third of the page lists items under the headings "Folio no. 1[-3]". The bottom portion includes lists headed by size designations: A.1, B.2, C.3, and D.4.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink.

did.unittitle: Mt. Sinai sent to John E. Cross, Feby. 1886
did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 31 x 22 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists 70 mounts / 115 drawings, with subjects including Cairo and Mt. Sinai.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet ; 31 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The title words "in published journals" are added in graphite. The list comprises named places in Corsica (without dates). The contents appear to correspond to the illustrations that were published in: Journal of a landscape painter in Corsica, by Edward Lear (London: Robert John Bush, 1870).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Written in black ink, with additions in graphite.

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet (4 pages) ; 33 x 42 cm, folded to 33 x 21 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A chronological listing of Lear's oil paintings, numbered 1-292, giving the subject of each and the name of the person for whom each painting was made or who purchased it. Lacks imprint.

did.physdesc: 1 clipping ; 62 x 18 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clipping from the August 15, 1896, issue of The Isle of Wight County Press, containing an obituary for Emily, Lady Tennyson.

did.physdesc: 1 sheet : illustrations ; 45 x 31 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Auden's poem, framed by a pastiche of Lear's art by Bip Pares, first appeared in the Times Literary Supplement Spring Books supplement, 25 March 1939. The border is signed "Bip Pares after Edward Lear." A manuscript of the poem, in Auden's hand, is also present in the collection (see Box 2, f. 1).

did.physdesc: 1 folded sheet (4 pages) ; 28 x 22 cm, folded to 14 x 11 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed checklist by the dealer George Dix, for works by Lear on display, December 2, 1947-January 3, 1948. The list includes the names of lenders, including Donald Gallup.

did.unittitle: St. Kiven and the Gentle Kathleen
did.physdesc: 24 pages : illustrations ; 13 x 18 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The publication reproduces images of the manuscript found in the present collection (see box 1, f. 12), with a transcription of the text and an introduction by Donald Gallup.
"Calligraphy by John O.C. McCrillis. Printed by John Henry's, North Haven, Connecticut. 500 copies. No. [76]. 300 copies destroyed and 100 copies reissued 3 June 1985."--Colophon.

title.part: Moore, Thomas, 1779-1852. By that lake, whose gloomy shore
